code: @Manual{,   title = {wf: Artificial Intelligence Workflow Tools},   author = {Christopher T. Kenny},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.1},   url = {https://christophertkenny.com/wf/}, }

title: Artificial Intelligence Workflow Tools
text: wf manages skills AI coding agents. Skills reusable instruction sets extend agent can . live conventional directory agent (e.g., .claude/skills/ Claude Code) can installed GitHub local path.

title: Artificial Intelligence Workflow Tools
code: # install.packages('pak') pak::pak('christopherkenny/wf')

title: Artificial Intelligence Workflow Tools
text: Set WF_AGENT .Renviron every wf function knows agent use: Add line like: Supported agents: 'claude_code' (alias: 'claude'), 'openclaw', 'codex', 'cursor', 'gemini_cli', 'github_copilot' (alias: 'copilot'), 'posit_ai' (alias: 'posit'). Skills can scoped project (.agent/skills/) installed globally (~/.agent/skills/):
code: usethis::edit_r_environ() WF_AGENT='claude_code' library(wf)  skill_path('claude_code', 'project') # project-local #> [1] ".claude/skills" skill_path('claude_code', 'global') # user-global #> [1] "~/.claude/skills" skill_path() # auto-detected from WF_AGENT or directory #> [1] ".claude/skills"

title: Artificial Intelligence Workflow Tools
text: Install skill GitHub using owner/repo shorthand full URL: repos bundle multiple skills, use skill argument: List, check, update installed skills:
code: add_skill('some-user/some-skill') add_skill('some-user/skills', skill = 'proofread') list_skills() #>                                                           name #> .claude/skills/tidy-argument-checking              types-check #> .claude/skills/tidy-deprecate-function tidy-deprecate-function #>                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             description #> .claude/skills/tidy-argument-checking  Validate function inputs in R using a standalone file of check_* functions. Use when writing exported R functions that need input validation, reviewing existing validation code, or when creating new input validation helpers. #> .claude/skills/tidy-deprecate-function                    Guide for deprecating R functions/arguments. Use when a user asks to deprecate a function or parameter, including adding lifecycle warnings, updating documentation, adding NEWS entries, and updating tests. #>                                        source installed_at #> .claude/skills/tidy-argument-checking    <NA>         <NA> #> .claude/skills/tidy-deprecate-function   <NA>         <NA> check_skills() #> [1] name             installed_sha    latest_sha       update_available #> <0 rows> (or 0-length row.names) update_skills() #> All skills are up to date.

title: Artificial Intelligence Workflow Tools
text: Search GitHub community skills tagged claude-skill: Scaffold new skill template SKILL.md:
code: find_skill()               # all skills find_skill('quarto')   # filtered by keyword tmp <- tempfile() init_skill('my-skill', tmp) #> Created skill "my-skill" at #> 'C:/Users/chris/AppData/Local/Temp/Rtmp2tcpxX/file7af05103d6c/my-skill'.

title: Install a skill — add_skill
text: Installs skill GitHub repository local directory skills directory.

title: Install a skill — add_skill
code: add_skill(source, path = NULL, skill = NULL, overwrite = FALSE)

title: Install a skill — add_skill
text: source One : GitHub URL pointing repo, e.g. "https://github.com/owner/repo". GitHub URL pointing subdirectory, e.g. "https://github.com/owner/repo/tree/main/path//skill". GitHub shorthand, e.g. "owner/repo". local directory path containing SKILL.md file. path skills directory install . Can one : known agent name "claude_code", "cursor", "github_copilot" (see skill_path() full list) use agent's conventional project-scope path. character string giving directory path directly. NULL (default), case path resolved WF_AGENT environment variable, prompting interactive sessions. Set WF_AGENT .Renviron (e.g. usethis::edit_r_environ()) avoid prompt. skill multi-skill repositories store skills skills/ subdirectory, name skill install, e.g. skill = "proofread". supplied, skill read skills/<skill> within repository. Ignored source already points specific subdirectory via /tree/.... overwrite FALSE (default), error raised skill already installed. Set TRUE replace .

title: Install a skill — add_skill
text: path installed skill directory, invisibly.

title: Install a skill — add_skill
code: src <- tempfile() dir.create(src) writeLines(   c('---', 'name: example', 'description: An example skill.', '---'),   file.path(src, 'SKILL.md') ) add_skill(src, tempfile()) #> Installed skill "example" to /tmp/RtmpkMG7in/file1980f5d309d/example.

what: Check installed skills for available updates — check_skills
title: Check installed skills for available updates — check_skills
text: Compares installed skill's recorded commit SHA latest commit GitHub. Local skills reported updatable.

title: Check installed skills for available updates — check_skills
code: check_skills(path = NULL)

title: Check installed skills for available updates — check_skills
text: path skills directory check. Can one : known agent name "claude_code", "cursor", "github_copilot" (see skill_path() full list) use agent's conventional project-scope path. character string giving directory path directly. NULL (default), case path resolved WF_AGENT environment variable, prompting interactive sessions. Set WF_AGENT .Renviron (e.g. usethis::edit_r_environ()) avoid prompt.

title: Check installed skills for available updates — check_skills
text: data frame columns: name: skill name. installed_sha: SHA recorded install time (NA local). latest_sha: current HEAD SHA GitHub (NA local network failure). update_available: TRUE installed latest SHAs differ.

title: Check installed skills for available updates — check_skills
code: check_skills(tempfile()) #> [1] name             installed_sha    latest_sha       update_available #> <0 rows> (or 0-length row.names)

what: Search for skills on GitHub — find_skill
title: Search for skills on GitHub — find_skill
text: Searches GitHub repositories tagged skill topic matching keyword query. Searches across supported agent topic conventions (e.g. claude-skill, cursor-skill).

title: Search for skills on GitHub — find_skill
code: find_skill(query = NULL)

title: Search for skills on GitHub — find_skill
text: query Keyword search .

title: Search for skills on GitHub — find_skill
text: data frame columns: name: repository name. description: repository description. owner: repository owner login. url: full URL repository. stars: number GitHub stars.

title: Search for skills on GitHub — find_skill
code: find_skill('rstats') #> [1] name        description owner       url         stars       #> <0 rows> (or 0-length row.names)

what: Create a new skill template — init_skill
title: Create a new skill template — init_skill
text: Creates new skill directory path/name/ containing template SKILL.md file ready filled .

title: Create a new skill template — init_skill
code: init_skill(name, path)

title: Create a new skill template — init_skill
text: name Skill name. Must 1-64 characters, lowercase alphanumeric single hyphens (consecutive --), start end hyphen. Consider using gerund form (e.g. "parsing-logs"). path Directory create skill. skill directory path/name. Can known agent name "claude_code" "github_copilot" use agent's conventional project-scope path (see skill_path() full list).

title: Create a new skill template — init_skill
text: path new skill directory, invisibly.

title: Create a new skill template — init_skill
code: init_skill('my-skill', tempfile()) #> Created skill "my-skill" at /tmp/RtmpkMG7in/file19806f6b7c05/my-skill.

title: List installed skills — list_skills
text: Returns data frame describing skills installed skills directory.

title: List installed skills — list_skills
code: list_skills(path = NULL)

title: List installed skills — list_skills
text: path skills directory inspect. Can one : known agent name "claude_code", "cursor", "github_copilot" (see skill_path() full list) use agent's conventional project-scope path. character string giving directory path directly. NULL (default), case path resolved WF_AGENT environment variable, prompting interactive sessions. Set WF_AGENT .Renviron (e.g. usethis::edit_r_environ()) avoid prompt.

title: List installed skills — list_skills
text: data frame columns: name: skill name SKILL.md frontmatter. description: skill description SKILL.md frontmatter. source: source URL local path skill installed . installed_at: ISO 8601 timestamp skill installed.

title: List installed skills — list_skills
code: list_skills(tempfile()) #> [1] name         description  source       installed_at #> <0 rows> (or 0-length row.names)

title: Remove an installed skill — remove_skill
text: Deletes skill directory skills directory removes lock file.

title: Remove an installed skill — remove_skill
code: remove_skill(name, path = NULL, force = FALSE)

title: Remove an installed skill — remove_skill
text: name name skill remove. path skills directory skill installed. Can one : known agent name "claude_code", "cursor", "github_copilot" (see skill_path() full list) use agent's conventional project-scope path. character string giving directory path directly. NULL (default), case path resolved WF_AGENT environment variable, prompting interactive sessions. Set WF_AGENT .Renviron (e.g. usethis::edit_r_environ()) avoid prompt. force FALSE (default), prompts confirmation interactive sessions. Set TRUE skip prompt.

title: Remove an installed skill — remove_skill
text: name removed skill, invisibly.

title: Remove an installed skill — remove_skill
code: src <- tempfile() dir.create(src) writeLines(   c('---', 'name: example', 'description: An example skill.', '---'),   file.path(src, 'SKILL.md') ) tmp <- tempfile() add_skill(src, tmp) #> Installed skill "example" to /tmp/RtmpkMG7in/file198092ef69b/example. remove_skill('example', tmp, force = TRUE) #> Removed skill "example" from /tmp/RtmpkMG7in/file198092ef69b.

what: Get the conventional skill path for an agent — skill_path
title: Get the conventional skill path for an agent — skill_path
text: Returns conventional directory path skills given agent stored. path expanded (.e., ~ resolved home directory). Use base::path.expand() need absolute path.

title: Get the conventional skill path for an agent — skill_path
code: skill_path(agent = NULL, scope = c("project", "global"))

title: Get the conventional skill path for an agent — skill_path
text: agent One "claude_code" (alias "claude"), "openclaw", "codex", "cursor", "gemini_cli", "github_copilot" (alias "copilot"), "posit_ai" (alias "posit"). NULL (default), agent resolved order: (1) WF_AGENT environment variable, (2) scan current working directory recognised agent config folder (.claude, .cursor, etc.), (3) final fallback "claude_code". Set WF_AGENT .Renviron (e.g. usethis::edit_r_environ()) avoid passing agent every time. scope One "project" (path relative current working directory, suitable committing version control) "global" (path user's home directory, available across projects).

title: Get the conventional skill path for an agent — skill_path
text: length-1 character vector giving conventional skill path.

title: Get the conventional skill path for an agent — skill_path
code: skill_path('claude_code', 'project') #> [1] ".claude/skills" skill_path('claude', 'project') # alias for claude_code #> [1] ".claude/skills" skill_path('github_copilot', 'project') #> [1] ".copilot/skills" skill_path('copilot', 'project') # alias for github_copilot #> [1] ".copilot/skills" skill_path('posit_ai', 'project') #> [1] ".positai/skills" skill_path('posit', 'project') # alias for posit_ai #> [1] ".positai/skills" skill_path('cursor', 'global') #> [1] "~/.cursor/skills" skill_path() # auto-detects from WF_AGENT, dir scan, or falls back to claude_code #> [1] ".claude/skills"

title: Update installed skills — update_skills
text: Checks installed skill available updates re-installs newer version GitHub.

title: Update installed skills — update_skills
code: update_skills(path = NULL)

title: Update installed skills — update_skills
text: path skills directory update. Can one : known agent name "claude_code", "cursor", "github_copilot" (see skill_path() full list) use agent's conventional project-scope path. character string giving directory path directly. NULL (default), case path resolved WF_AGENT environment variable, prompting interactive sessions. Set WF_AGENT .Renviron (e.g. usethis::edit_r_environ()) avoid prompt.

title: Update installed skills — update_skills
text: character vector updated skill names, invisibly.

title: Update installed skills — update_skills
code: update_skills(tempfile()) #> All skills are up to date.

what: wf: Artificial Intelligence Workflow Tools — wf-package
title: wf: Artificial Intelligence Workflow Tools — wf-package
text: Manage skills large language model coding agents. Supports installing skills GitHub local directories, tracking versions lock file, keeping installations current. Installations can scoped single project shared globally across projects.

title: wf: Artificial Intelligence Workflow Tools — wf-package
text: Maintainer: Christopher T. Kenny ctkenny@proton.(ORCID)

title: wf 0.0.1
text: add_skill(), check_skills(), find_skill(), init_skill(), list_skills(), remove_skill(), update_skills() new functions managing AI agent skills. add_skill(), check_skills(), list_skills(), remove_skill(), update_skills() now default path NULL. NULL, path resolved WF_AGENT environment variable, interactive prompt listing detected agent directories. Passing known agent name (e.g., "github_copilot") path now also supported shorthand agent’s conventional project-scope path. skill_path() now supports "copilot" alias "github_copilot". skill_path() now supports Posit AI ("posit_ai", alias "posit") base directory .positai.
